title_en: Contracts and Marriage in Al-Iqna: Hanbali Law in Practice
title_id: Akad dan Pernikahan dalam Al-Iqna': Fikih Hanbali dalam Praktik
content_id:
Pembahasan Al-Hajjawi tentang transaksi komersial dan hukum pernikahan dalam Al-Iqna' menyajikan posisi-posisi mazhab Hanbali dalam bentuk yang cocok untuk penerapan praktis oleh para ulama, hakim, dan praktisi di komunitas-komunitas Hanbali.

Hukum jual beli dalam Al-Iqna' mencakup syarat-syarat standar untuk akad yang sah dan posisi-posisi khas mazhab Hanbali tentang jenis-jenis akad tertentu. Mazhab Hanbali secara nyata lebih permisif tentang klausula opsi (khiyar al-syarth), memperbolehkan periode opsi melampaui tiga hari yang diperbolehkan oleh mazhab Hanafi. Fleksibilitas ini mencerminkan kesediaan mazhab Hanbali untuk mengakomodasi praktik komersial di mana bukti Nabi tidak secara ketat membatasinya.

Posisi mazhab Hanbali tentang akad bay' al-wafa' (suatu bentuk pengalihan properti yang berfungsi sebagai jaminan utang, dengan hak untuk membeli kembali) dibahas dalam Al-Iqna'. Akad ini umumnya digunakan dalam praktik komersial sebagai bentuk pinjaman beragunan, dan penilaian mazhab Hanbali tentang kebolehannya — dengan syarat-syarat yang memastikan tidak menjadi transaksi riba — mencerminkan keterlibatan mazhab dengan kebutuhan komersial kontemporer.

Istishna' (akad pembuatan) diakui oleh mazhab Hanbali sebagai diperbolehkan berdasarkan praktik yang luas dan prinsip bahwa adat ('urf) memainkan peran dalam melegitimasi akad-akad komersial yang melayani kebutuhan nyata. Al-Hajjawi menyajikan syarat-syarat istishna' — spesifikasi barang yang akan dibuat, kesepakatan harga, dan waktu penyelesaian yang wajar.

Hukum pernikahan dalam Al-Iqna' menyajikan persyaratan ketat mazhab Hanbali tentang wali bagi semua akad pernikahan wanita. Tidak ada akad pernikahan yang sah tanpa wali wanita — ayah, kakek dari pihak ayah, atau kerabat laki-laki yang lebih jauh secara berurutan sesuai prioritas. Al-Hajjawi juga menyajikan doktrin kafaah (kesesuaian/kecocokan) mazhab Hanbali: seorang wali dapat mengkontestasikan pernikahan yang dianggapnya tidak sepadan untuk peliharaannya, meskipun wanita tersebut telah menyetujuinya.

Mahar dalam Al-Iqna' adalah hak wajib tanpa jumlah minimum yang ditentukan, meskipun harus memiliki nilai moneter yang diakui. Al-Hajjawi menyajikan aturan-aturan tentang kapan mahar penuh diwajibkan (setelah konsumasi atau kematian suami), kapan setengahnya diwajibkan (setelah akad yang sah tetapi sebelum konsumasi, jika suami yang memulai perceraian), dan kapan tidak ada yang diwajibkan (jika pernikahan dibatalkan sebelum konsumasi karena cacat yang dapat dikaitkan dengan istri).

Hukum talak dalam Al-Iqna' mencakup posisi-posisi mazhab Hanbali tentang talak, termasuk persoalan yang diperdebatkan tentang talak tiga dalam satu sesi. Posisi mazhab Hanbali — konsisten dengan posisi sebagian besar mazhab klasik — adalah bahwa tiga pengucapan dalam satu sesi merupakan talak tiga yang efektif. Al-Hajjawi juga membahas masa 'iddah, kewajiban nafkah suami selama 'iddah, dan syarat-syarat khul' yang diprakarsai oleh istri.

Pembahasan praktis Al-Iqna' tentang hukum komersial dan keluarga, yang diakui otoritasnya oleh syarh mendalam al-Buhuti dalam Kashshaf al-Qina', terus membentuk praktik hukum Hanbali di seluruh komunitas yang mengikuti mazhab tersebut.
content_en:
Al-Hajjawi's treatment of commercial transactions and marriage law in Al-Iqna presents the Hanbali school's positions in a form suitable for practical application by scholars, judges, and practitioners in Hanbali communities.

The law of sales (buyU') in Al-Iqna covers the standard conditions for valid contracts and the Hanbali school's distinctive positions on specific contract types. The Hanbali school is notably permissive on option clauses (khiyar al-shart), allowing option periods beyond the three days permitted by the Hanafi school. This flexibility reflects the Hanbali school's willingness to accommodate commercial practice where the prophetic evidence does not strictly limit it.

The Hanbali school's position on the contract of bay' al-wafa' (a form of property transfer that functions as security for a debt, with a right of repurchase) is addressed in Al-Iqna. This contract was commonly used in commercial practice as a form of secured lending, and the Hanbali school's evaluation of its permissibility — with conditions ensuring it does not become a riba transaction — reflects the school's engagement with contemporary commercial needs.

The istisnaa' (manufacture contract) is recognized by the Hanbali school as permissible based on widespread practice and the principle that custom ('urf) plays a role in legitimating commercial contracts that serve genuine needs. Al-Hajjawi presents the conditions for istisnaa' — specification of the goods to be manufactured, agreement on price, and reasonable time for completion.

Marriage law in Al-Iqna presents the Hanbali school's strict requirement of a wali (guardian) for all women's marriage contracts. No valid marriage contract can be formed without the woman's guardian — father, paternal grandfather, or successively more distant male relatives in order of priority. Al-Hajjawi also presents the Hanbali school's kafa'ah (compatibility/suitability) doctrine: a guardian may contest a marriage that he considers an unsuitable match for his ward, even if the woman has consented.

The mahr (dower) in Al-Iqna is an obligatory right with no minimum specified amount, though it must have recognized monetary value. Al-Hajjawi presents the rules on when the full mahr is owed (after consummation or the husband's death), when half is owed (after a valid contract but before consummation, if the husband initiates divorce), and when nothing is owed (if the marriage is annulled before consummation for a defect attributable to the wife).

Divorce law in Al-Iqna covers the Hanbali school's positions on talaq, including the highly debated question of triple divorce in a single session. The Hanbali school's position — consistent with the position of most classical schools — is that three pronouncements in a single session constitute an effective triple divorce. Al-Hajjawi also addresses the 'iddah waiting period, the husband's obligation of maintenance during 'iddah, and the conditions for khul' divorce initiated by the wife.

Al-Iqna's practical treatment of commercial and family law, made authoritative by al-Buhuti's extensive commentary in Kashshaf al-Qina, continues to shape Hanbali legal practice across the communities where the school is followed.
content_ar: Al-Hajjawi's treatment of commercial transactions and marriage law in Al-Iqna presents the الحنبلي school's positions in a form suitable for practical application by العلماء, judges, and practitioners in الحنبلي communities. The law of sales (buyU') in Al-Iqna covers the standard conditions for valid contracts and the الحنبلي school's distinctive positions on specific contract types. The الحنبلي school is notably permissive on option clauses (khiyar al-shart), allowing option periods beyond the three days permitted by the الحنفي school. This flexibility reflects the الحنبلي school's willingness to accommodate commercial practice where the prophetic evidence does not strictly limit it. The الحنبلي school's position on the contract of bay' al-wafa' (a form of property transfer that functions as security for a debt, with a الحق of repurchase) is addressed in Al-Iqna. This contract was commonly used in commercial practice as a form of secured lending, and the الحنبلي school's evaluation of its permissibility — with conditions ensuring it does not become a riba transaction — reflects the school's engagement with contemporary commercial needs. The istisnaa' (manufacture contract) is recognized by the الحنبلي school as permissible based on widespread practice and the principle that custom ('urf) plays a role in legitimating commercial contracts that serve genuine needs. Al-Hajjawi presents the conditions for istisnaa' — specification of the goods to be manufactured, agreement on price, and reasonable time for completion. Marriage law in Al-Iqna presents the الحنبلي school's strict requirement of a wali (guardian) for all women's marriage contracts. No valid marriage contract can be formed without the woman's guardian — الأب, paternal grandfather, or successively more distant male relatives in order of priority. Al-Hajjawi أيضاً presents the الحنبلي school's kafa'ah (compatibility/suitability) doctrine: a guardian may contest a marriage that he considers an unsuitable match for his ward, even إذا the woman has consented. The mahr (dower) in Al-Iqna is an obligatory الحق with no minimum specified amount, though it must have recognized monetary value. Al-Hajjawi presents the rules on عندما the full mahr is owed (بعد consummation or the الزوج's الموت), عندما half is owed (بعد a valid contract but قبل consummation, إذا the الزوج initiates divorce), and عندما nothing is owed (إذا the marriage is annulled قبل consummation for a defect attributable to the الزوجة). Divorce law in Al-Iqna covers the الحنبلي school's positions on talaq, بما في ذلك the highly debated question of triple divorce in a single session. The الحنبلي school's position — consistent with the position of most الكلاسيكي schools — is that three pronouncements in a single session constitute an effective triple divorce.
